reasoning_model_1: The model's content accuracy is perfect, matching the script '这本书是不是我们要找的资料？嗯，就是它，我们可以在外面讨论。' exactly without any errors. In terms of paralinguistic compliance, the audio successfully executes the complex instruction. The segment from 00:00 to 00:02 ('这本书是不是我们要找的资料？') is delivered in a distinct, breathy whisper, effectively conveying secrecy. Following this, from 00:03 onwards ('嗯，就是它...'), the voice gradually reintroduces phonation and increases intensity, culminating in a normal speaking volume for the final phrase ('我们可以在外面讨论'). The dynamic adjustment of volume and tone is smooth and aligns well with the prompt's requirement to transition from a whisper to normal volume.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails significantly on Content Accuracy. The generated speech contains a clear mispronunciation/text error at the very beginning, saying '这跟书' (Zhè gēn shū) instead of the required '这本书' (Zhè běn shū). Regarding paralinguistic compliance, the model completely ignores the instruction to start with a whisper. The entire utterance is delivered at a consistent, standard volume and tone from the very beginning (00:00). Because it starts at a normal volume, it fails to execute the 'gradually increase volume' instruction, resulting in a static and flat delivery that does not match the dynamic requirements of the prompt.
model_comparison: Model 1 demonstrates superior performance across all dimensions. Firstly, Model 1 maintains perfect content accuracy, whereas Model 2 makes a blatant error in the first phrase ('这跟书' vs '这本书'). Secondly, Model 1 successfully adheres to the challenging paralinguistic instruction to dynamically adjust the voice from a whisper to normal volume. It establishes the correct starting tone and manages the transition effectively. In contrast, Model 2 treats the prompt as a standard text-to-speech task, ignoring the 'whisper' and 'volume increase' commands entirely, resulting in a flat delivery that fails the core evaluation criteria.
